chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – Wiping the head and the two feet

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/105)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/105)
text: الَّذِي يَخْضِبُ رَأْسَهُ بِالْحِنَّاءِ ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ فِي الْوُضُوءِ قَالَ لَا يَجُوزُ حَتَّى يُصِيبَ بَشَرَةَ رَأْسِهِ بِالْمَاءِ .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) raising it from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the one who dyes his head with the henna, then it becomes inevitable for him with regards to the Ablution (Wudhu). Heasws said: ‘He is not allowed until he pours in the hair of his head with the water’ (attached to his hands).
en.sarwar: 12. Muhammad ibn Yahya in a marfu‘ manner has narrated from the following: “About a man who dyes his head with henna and notices it during Wudu’, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, has said, ‘It is not permissible until water reaches the skin of his head.’”
